about.label: About
about.heading: Who I am
about.paragraph: I'm Leonardo Pottmayer, a full stack software engineer from Pomerode, SC — Brazil. Passionate about software since 2019, I focus on building robust, scalable solutions that generate real business impact. I hold a Bachelor's in Computer Science from FURB, where my thesis on applying computer vision to disease diagnosis contributed to advancing the field and earned a perfect score. Naturally curious, driven by continuous learning.

quicksoft_pleno.intro: In a role of greater technical responsibility, I took on more complex demands with increasing autonomy, maintaining involvement across all product projects and contributing to new projects, process improvements, and technical decisions.
bullets: Integrations: Implemented integrations with multiple financial market systems, including Brazilian banks with PIX support, connecting the ERP to external systems to automate steps in the receivables anticipation flow.

Code review: Took on the role of code reviewer and technical reference for the team, being regularly consulted by colleagues on architecture and design decisions, contributing to standardization and delivery quality.

quicksoft_junior.intro: Full stack development on the legacy ERP (Qprof) and on projects with a modern stack, with growing technical ownership and involvement in system design decisions. Throughout the period, I delivered integrations, APIs, and improvements across multiple product fronts.
bullets: ERP Qprof: Reduced ERP Qprof's debug startup time by ~75% (from ~4 to ~1 minute) by converting unnecessary synchronous routines to asynchronous and optimizing high-cost routines, boosting development team productivity.

New Project: Participated in the development of the company's first project using hexagonal architecture and multi-tenancy with .NET 7/8, React, TypeScript, Docker, and Redis — from design to implementation, serving as the technical foundation for ERP modernization.

ERP Modernization: Took technical ownership of the ERP Qprof modernization toward a microservices architecture, contributing to implementation and system design decisions that defined the structure and ensured long-term scalability and maintainability.

quicksoft_aprendiz.intro: First professional experience in the technology field, learning in practice how software development works in a real product. Worked on developing and maintaining platform features using ASP.NET Web Forms applications and Oracle PL/SQL routines.

furb.description: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, graduated December 2025. Thesis developed a model for analyzing neuron microscopy images, detecting and counting atypical neurons through morphological changes — a study that contributes to the field of disease detection via computer vision. Achieved good results and was awarded a perfect score, evaluated by professors from both Computer Science and Biomedicine. Solid curriculum covering CS fundamentals — algorithms, data structures, compilers, computer architecture, and information security — alongside applied disciplines in software engineering, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and image processing.

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.subheading: Interested in new challenges where I can keep growing, contribute to the team, and generate real product impact. Feel free to reach out.

footer.quote: The mind that opens to a new idea never returns to its original size.

seo.title: Leonardo Pottmayer – Full Stack Software Engineer
seo.description: Full stack software engineer from Brazil specializing in .NET, React, TypeScript and Oracle. CS graduate from FURB (2025), currently Software Engineer at Quick Soft Tecnologia.
